plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-335 RP10.P07.M04.S03-RP10.P07.M05.S20 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-335 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP10.P07 failure binding slice: the M04 secondary workflow tail (twenty failure rows from missing actor failure through human escalation note) and the M05 permission and audit binding head (twenty failure rows from failure taxonomy through Hermes failure evidence) across two sections over 40 planned rows with a pack-local row-extras constant, Hermes evidence (H10), Claude review packet (C10), and CP00-336 handoff, without opening rollback/retry runtime, lock acquisition, test runtime paths, Hermes runtime receipts, object storage, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-335 is a descriptor-only RP10 intake-core "failure binding slice" pack (risk B, 40 units, RP10.P07.M04.S03-RP10.P07.M05.S20) covering the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (20 failure rows) and M05 Permission And Audit Binding head (20 failure rows). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is fully supported and honest: pack identity, deliverable_distribution {failure_recovery:22,security_audit:4,implementation:3,hermes_evidence:4,fixture:2,test:4,claude_review:1}, and micro_title_distribution all reconcile to 40 and match across baseline, manifest (registry binding registry.js:249), contract projection, and validator script. Implementation (service.js:2239 createIntakeCoreCp335FailureBindingSliceDescriptor + freezeCp335Result attestations) follows the established CP321-334 pattern. Validators (validators.js:3317/3352) enforce coverage, distribution, descriptor-only flags, every no-leak guard, authority boundary, contract drift, and handoff; the inherited ROW_EXTRAS chain (CP333->334->335) supplies every field the validator checks. Three dedicated CP335 tests (model.test.js:1014-1068) load the real contract/plan and assert validity, descriptor-only rows, claude_is_final_approval=false and human_final_approval_required=true. Command evidence shows all 22 commands exit 0. Authority boundary is correct: Claude is read-only non-final reviewer, human final approval required, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, and pack-level production_ready is honestly false (descriptor layer, runtime_ready false) — the gate is not weakened. Hermes is used only for deterministic evidence packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). Review-hardening evidence is valid: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1446-byte valid JSON result, non-empty stdout), prompt 2243B under the 25600B limit, no full diff/source embedded in prompt, read-only tools only, summary-only review correctly prohibited (Risk B + security/audit + review-evidence deliverables), and no active untracked implementation files omitted. Changed-file scope is clean; unrelated dirty files (docx, workbook, LDIP) are correctly preserved and excluded. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-335 intake core failure binding slice descriptor pack
